pr.title: feat: add ghost piece drop shadow projection

summary: Enhanced the ghost piece (drop shadow projection) with neon-style rendering including pulsing glow, dashed borders, and gradient fills. Extracted getGhostPosition() as a reusable utility and added G key toggle with UI hint.
changes: Extracted getGhostPosition() utility function for reusable landing position calculation
Enhanced drawGhost() with pulsing neon glow, dashed borders, gradient fills, and inner highlights
Added ghost piece toggle via G key with control hint in UI panel
Added ghostEnabled and ghostTime state variables
Added 8 new tests for ghost piece functionality

tests.details: 8 new tests cover: ghost position calculator, ghost renderer, toggle state, toggle key, pulsing animation, dashed border, neon glow effect, and UI control hint

tradeoffs.choice: Ghost renders every frame with sin-based animation
tradeoffs.reason: Provides smooth pulsing visual feedback matching the neon aesthetic
tradeoffs.downside: Minimal extra computation per frame, negligible on modern hardware

decisions.reason: Reusable for hard drop calculations and future features like ghost piece distance display

decisions.decision: Ghost enabled by default with G key toggle
decisions.reason: Most Tetris players expect ghost piece; toggle lets players who find it distracting disable it

decisions.decision: Dashed border instead of solid for ghost blocks
decisions.reason: Clearly distinguishes ghost from active piece while maintaining neon theme

notes: The ghost piece automatically updates when the current piece moves or rotates. It disappears when the piece is already at its landing position (no visual overlap).
